perturbation.original_text: 3.5. Due Date for Payment. Your Royalty payments and Advertising Contributions are due by the first (1st) day after the end of each Period (or the next business day if the first day is a Sunday or federal holiday) (as of the Agreement Date, the due date is Monday of each week). You must pay all other amounts due to us as specified in this Agreement or, if no time is specified, such amounts are due upon receipt of an invoice from us.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5. Due Date for Payment. Your Royalty payments and Advertising Contributions are due by the fifth (5th) day after the end of each Period (or the next business day if the fifth day is a Sunday or federal holiday) (as of the Agreement Date, the due date is Monday of each week). You must pay all other amounts due to us as specified in this Agreement or, if no time is specified, such amounts are due upon receipt of an invoice from us.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the due date for Royalty payments and Advertising Contributions from the 1st to the 5th day after the end of each Period. This contradicts with section 3.7, where the due date affects the calculation of late fees and interest, creating uncertainty about when penalties apply. Specifically the calculation of if a late fee should be applied. This creates an in-text contradiction as to whether interest should be applied from the first day or the fifth day, creating potential disputes over what terms should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7. Delinquency. If any Royalties or other amounts owed to us are not paid in full by the due date, we have the right to charge interest on the overdue amount at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month (or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, if less than 1.5%) from the date such amount was due until paid in full. Unpaid interest charges will compound annually. In addition, we will have the right to charge a late fee for each occurrence of a payment that is more than 30 days past due, which fee will be $100 for the first such occurrence, $200 for the second such occurrence, and $300 for the third and each subsequent occurrence during the Term of this Agreement. The late fee is to compensate us for our administrative costs incurred in enforcing your obligation to pay us.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the due date for royalty and advertising contributions from the 1st to the 5th day of the period. Section 3.7 specifies that interest is charged on overdue amounts from the 'due date'. The change directly creates a contradiction, because it introduces uncertainty as to whether the 'due date' for penalties calculation is the 1st or the 5th. This creates a contradiction as to when the interest should be applied, based on whether the first day or the fifth day is the actual due date, leading to potential disputes over contractual obligations, which fulfills the criteria for Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.3 Location Development and Preparation. You must construct, furnish, and open the Franchised Business as required by this Agreement and must open the Franchised Business not later than six (6) months after securing the necessary authorization and approval for permits and/or certificates. Time is of the essence.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 Location Development and Preparation. You must construct, furnish, and open the Franchised Business as required by this Agreement and must open the Franchised Business not later than twelve (12) months after securing the necessary authorization and approval for permits and/or certificates. Time is of the essence.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the deadline for opening the Franchised Business from six (6) months to twelve (12) months after securing necessary permits. This in-text contradicts section 15.2.2, which refers to Appendix A for the opening deadline, because although this is not explicitly stated in the perturbation section itself, but it is an opening deadline that can be modified and updated in Appendix A. This creates an uncertainty on which deadline is valid, and leads to disputes in the contract regarding enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.2.2. You fail to open for business by the opening deadline specified in Appendix A.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the deadline for opening the Franchised Business from six to twelve months. This directly contradicts section 15.2.2, which refers to Appendix A for the opening deadline. Since Appendix A could specify the original six-month deadline, the change creates a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding which deadline is valid, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.19.1. Types and Amounts of Coverage. Throughout the entire Term, you must maintain such types of insurance, in such amounts, as we may require. Such insurance is in addition to any other insurance that may be required by applicable law, your landlord, or otherwise. Policies that we require must be written by an insurance company reasonably satisfactory to us with an A.M. Best rating of "A" or better, and, must name us as an additional insured party. At a minimum, such policies must include the following: (a) commercial general liability insurance, completed-operations and independent-contractors coverage in the amount of $1,000,000, per person/per occurrence for bodily injury and property damage combined with a general aggregate of $3,000,000.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.19.1. Types and Amounts of Coverage. Throughout the entire Term, you must maintain such types of insurance, in such amounts, as we may require. Such insurance is in addition to any other insurance that may be required by applicable law, your landlord, or otherwise. Policies that we require must be written by an insurance company reasonably satisfactory to us with an A.M. Best rating of "B" or better, and, must name us as an additional insured party. At a minimum, such policies must include the following: (a) commercial general liability insurance, completed-operations and independent-contractors coverage in the amount of $500,000, per person/per occurrence for bodily injury and property damage combined with a general aggregate of $1,000,000.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the minimum insurance requirements, specifically decreasing both the acceptable A.M. Best rating and the required coverage amounts. This directly contradicts section 6.19.2 which requires proof of compliance with section 6.19, and the contradiction also affects Section 15.2.14 regarding termination for failure to maintain required insurance. Now due to the change, the requirements of rating must be "B" or better, and the required coverage is in the amount of $500,000, per person/per occurrence for bodily injury and property damage combined with a general aggregate of $1,000,000. this results into uncertainty on which terms should be enforced, and can lead to disputes and litigation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.19.2. Evidence of Insurance. By the dates specified below, an approved insurance company must issue a certificate of insurance showing compliance with the insurance requirements in this Section 6.19 [Insurance] and you must furnish us with a paid receipt showing the certificate number: (a) 30 days before beginning construction of the Premises; (b) if the Premises are constructed and presently owned or leased by you, 10 days from the Agreement Date; or (c) if the Premises are not presently owned or leased, 10 days after ownership of the Premises is conveyed to you or you sign a lease for the Premises. The certificate of insurance must include a statement by the insurer that the policy or policies may not be canceled, subject to nonrenewal, or materially altered without at least 30 days' prior written notice to us. Upon our request, you must supply us with copies of all insurance policies and proof of payment. Every year, you must send us current certificates of insurance and copies of all insurance policies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 6.19.1, decreasing both the minimum acceptable A.M. Best rating (from "A" to "B") and the required coverage amounts. Section 6.19.2 requires that the certificate of insurance shows compliance with the insurance requirements in Section 6.19, creating a contradiction because the amended Section 6.19.1 now has different, less stringent requirements than what was initially intended and must be complied with.
